meanings.definition: a descent as through liquid (especially through water)
examples: they still talk about the sinking of the Titanic

meanings.definition: a slow fall or decline (as for lack of strength)
examples: after several hours of sinking an unexpected rally rescued the market
he could not control the sinking of his legs

meanings.definition: a feeling caused by uneasiness or apprehension
examples: a sinking feeling in the pit of my stomach

meanings.definition: fall or descend to a lower place or level

examples: The Japanese sank American ships in Pearl Harbor

meanings.definition: pass into a specified state or condition

examples: The raft sank and its occupants drowned

meanings.definition: descend into or as if into some soft substance or place

examples: The sun dipped below the horizon
The setting sun sank below the tree line

meanings.definition: fall heavily or suddenly; decline markedly
examples: The real estate market fell off
She sank her fingers into the soft sand
He buried his head in her lap
